transcript.text: A week in and of itself.
How's your experience been?
Slightly different, but good.
It's still good.
Starting a new year, new energy.
New year, new me.
This meeting is being recorded.
I actually remembered your name from when we were emailing before you took this job, but you had a chance to connect with Marcel, right?
did y'all ever connect?
He was way too busy and just gave up.
Oh, dang it.
I'm sorry to hear that.
No, no, It's so good.
We just have a connection that was really insisting that it would be super helpful to get a Marcel opinion regarding something, but that's fine.
You figured out your next step in your career anyways.
I figured out my Cool.
Well, thanks for the context and super helpful to kind of know what questions you're thinking through.
Did you need like a proper introduction and overview on what we do at GrowthX?
I wasn't sure how much context you had.
Okay, cool.
Awesome.
Let's start there and then we can dig more into like the specific opportunity.
So, GrowthX, think of us as like an end-to-end partner and extension of your content team.
The goal is definitely to help you get more visibility with traditional and AI search.
We'll focus on, you know, both sides, but we understand that AI search is where everyone's attention is at and it's more likely to drive conversions, right, and engaged visitors.
So, you know, what we're doing here at GrowthX is we're typically starting engagements with the strategy sprint where we'll identify a roadmap of opportunities.
We'll do all the keyword analysis and create topic clusters for all the content that we're going to create.
And then we'll start.
We publish some calibration articles so that you can see the quality we hit before we move into this Phase 2 where we really focus on scale.
So in Phase 2, we're doing five articles per week.
And throughout the entire engagement, we're doing all of the LLM monitoring and visibility for you.
And we're basically leveraging the data to help us define and pivot the strategy that we go down with content execution.
But that was a mouthful.
think in short, you know, we are an extension of your content team, which I know you haven't really built out yet.
So you don't have to worry about, you know, the scale that's needed to start to build domain authority as an early stage startup.
Any questions high level?
We'll dive deeper, obviously, but...
Let's dive deeper.
Perfect.
So I guess, do you know about like Marcel's background?
Yeah.
That would be helpful to touch on.
Okay.
Okay, cool.

So, yeah, I mean, basically built organic content engines at every step in his career and started GrowthX after having a lot of success building complex workflows at DeepGram and 24xing their traffic.

So I think, you know, the difference in terms of like why you might choose us over choosing a platform or choosing a different agency is really the idea that you don't have to wait so long to orchestrate workflows, learn how to use a tool, and you don't have to work with an agency that maybe does like a piece of this, but not the whole pie.
And so what we're doing is we're leveraging our internal platform, which I'll show you so that you can see like how the workflows are built and what they look like.
And a team of experts that come from backgrounds, basically doing SEO and GEO for logos that you see here.
You have 90 people in your agency or is it more of like freelancers and...
Yeah, I guess 90 plus in the whole company.
So this is like the whole company.
The delivery team is about 60, 65.
So it's a big proportion of the team.
And like, for example, on sales, like, I'm the only person.
So we don't have a lot of people in areas outside of the delivery that we're doing.
Okay.
And our entire delivery org, it's led by Matthew Panzarino, who was the editor-in-chief of TechCrunch for over a decade.
So we really do believe, like, not just in AI as the solution to create this content.
We want to have a human in the loop for every step of the process.
In terms of one question you brought up, you know, how sophisticated can you get with the content?
We're working with a ton of very technical clients.
Redis is a great example.
Abnormal Security, Sentinel One, Surge AI, Okta.
You get the idea.
There are a lot of clients who struggle with this problem.
What do I need to do in order to help you generate the best content?
Yeah, that was a great question.

So I would say really upfront, right, having resources for us that help us see how you're thinking about the personas that you're targeting, how you're positioning your product in the market, brand documents.
So what's the voice and tone that you're going for?
Examples of content that you really think is great.
Maybe the competitors created it, right, because I know there's not a ton of content on the Daylight website today.
These help us align on expectations and help us become experts on your business before we even start the engagement.
And then I would say in phase one, we want to calibrate as much as possible or we want to collaborate as much as possible so that we can.
Hit your quality bar early and then move towards creating articles that we would stage and publish.
So I think those are the biggest pieces, yeah.
All right.
So it's all AI-based.
There's no, I mean, there are editors in the loop to make sure that it's good content, readable, et cetera, that we're telling a story.
But all of the technical information is coming from the resources that I will supply and from the research, right?
We have managing editors who are technical.
So we would probably staff a technical managing editor on your account, or we would, not probably.
We would staff one of the more technical managing editors.
But, you know, we have the ability, like for augment code is a good example.
If you look at their artifacts, you know, we have...
Less technical right here, an artifact around who are those personas that they're going after.
And this is really because we just want to understand who's this content being written for, what language do they use.
But then we also have this proofreader checklist that basically says for any of the research that we do, leverage these types of technical sources.
And we have code snippets in almost every article for Augment Code.
So as I go into the workflows, you'll see like we can get very technical with what we're writing and leveraging AI to help create.
And then we augment that by having someone who has written for a technical client in the past be your point of contact from the human side.
Okay.
Yeah.
But great question for sure.
And in terms of how we would work together.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So you have the ability to opt out at any point during phase one.
And the idea is that we move to publishing content as soon as we've calibrated on quality.
So it's not just strategy.
And then in phase two, we do five articles per week.
And for a startup like yourself, you know, we would go ahead and charge you guys 15K per month instead of 18K per month.
Of understanding like you're earlier in this journey and taking a leap of faith.
What about regarding the fixed fee for the phase one?
For sure.
Low end, you know, 30K would be the cost of the phase one.
Okay.
So for the phase one, we're calibrating, we're creating some content, but not high scale, right?
More about finding the tone, the content plan.
we're creating creating some content, we're we're creating we're some content
So what should I expect in the first eight weeks?
Yeah, for sure.
In terms of output.
In terms of output.
So for everything that's connected to like the roadmap and the content strategy, we are going to stand up this content OS is what we call it, where we're doing all of the keyword analysis.
We're connecting it to search volume and difficulty of ranking against competitor content.
And then we are deciding on topic clusters where we can concentrate all of these different assignments, the areas where you want to have authority.
It can be broad or very specific.
And then we're starting to create a big roadmap of all these different assignments that will tell you which ones we recommend.
But it's flexible in terms of how we collaborate.
So this is happening up front.
And then depending on how fast we calibrate, some clients, we have 10 articles published in their CMS.
And we do all the staging and publishing for you.
So you don't have to worry about that.
During the first eight weeks.
During the first eight weeks.
Really dependent on us getting to a place where we can start, right, to stage and publish.
If we're, you know, stuck on the calibration articles going back and forth, then we might get closer to five done by the end of the strategy sprint.
But our goal is to get 10 to 20 articles published by the end of that strategy sprint.
Okay.
Okay.
And you said that you're publishing it?
So you're connected to my web flow and you're in charge of the images and all of the setup and everything?
Yeah.
We'll do cover images with every article.
We can create some infographics if the team wants to leverage them and we'll go in.
And log in, stage and publish, and make sure the content is optimized in terms of all the meta tags.
Okay.
Regarding phase two, let's say that you're doing five posts, five articles, pieces a week.
Well, actually, for five a week, that's a lot.
I was thinking about, do I need to augment, but five pieces a week is, I don't think we need more than that.
Okay.
Sorry, I'm just reading through and trying to think about scenarios.
Okay.
Let's, so I think that the biggest question, and I already also wrote it in the email, what do you need for me?
You first said, and that made a lot of sense, and you need information for me to start with.
The market, the positioning, our story, everything.
market, marketing, the marketing, the marketing,
Something that you can put in the system and start learning about us.
Understood.
What else in doing the phase one and phase two would you need for me in order for it to be a very successful partnership?
Absolutely.
What you outlined is pretty much it.
You know, I think we would handle the heavy lifting on identifying the right strategy.

But after we've calibrated, we would basically just want to make sure we have a clear review and feedback process so that there isn't a bottleneck where we've got five drafts and we don't get feedback on them for, you know, multiple days.

I think if we can have a good loop for communication back and forth, our goal is to really free up a ton of time so that like your team can focus on strategic marketing initiatives or your annual content pieces or case studies with customers.
Customers, things that just wouldn't make sense for us to try to create.
Okay.
Any other places where we would need to align?
I mean, technical SEO, stuff like that.
Things that you would rely on me to do in order for us to succeed?
Sorry.
The one thing is just making sure you've got the template set up in the CMS for the place that you want us to publish.
And what we ask for is like the ability to insert a table into the content, if that's possible.
That helps with LLMs if we can leverage tables that are comparing, right, like different tools, for example, as just like one example.
If you did not want us, because sometimes this is the case, to post directly to the blog, what Augment did is...
That sounds good.
Okay.
Yep.
Augment stood up the slash guides because they wanted to compare it to the blog and also because they didn't want to like inundate their main site with too much content.
And we've published like 300 articles for them.

Um, so, you know, I think, um, just making sure like we're aligned on what we would typically be creating, you know, I think like some of this is more thought leadership in nature, but our goal is like to create the content that's going to help you start to get more visibility in search.
What are those key questions?
definitely.
I mean, all of the product announcement and thought leadership, that's going to be things that I will publish.
That's not something that I'm counting on you.
Okay.
Okay.
Um, what else should I know?
Hmm.
I think, um, yeah, maybe.
I could just show you a couple quick success stories and then show you how the workflows are leveraged with the artifacts, just so you have a clear understanding of...
I saw a video regarding the workflow, you mean with Aerobs?
We actually have our internal platform, so we don't use Aerobs.
We are building our own engine, yeah.
Okay.

Um, yeah, I mean, I think with Augment Code, just kind of showing you how, with the content that we've created, you know, a lot of this is actually, um, there's like a lot more articles than I saw last time.
Hold on one second.
Oh, it's because I'm on their blog.
That makes a lot of sense.
I'm not on the guides anymore.
Okay, here we go.
So, with a lot of the content, just kind of want to point out that we'll do all the cross-linking.
There's code snippets and the content we've created for Augment.
When they started working with us, they were...
We're around 20K monthly organic.

They care a lot about being the best enterprise AI coding tool.
And so before they were working with us, right, like Cursor had a lot more visibility.
GitHub, obviously tough to supplant quickly because they're so large.
But what I love about this is that, you there's citations from articles that we've created.
Listed in their description.
And you're starting to see that, you know, it's both traditional search.
We're number two in one of the keywords that they care about a lot.
And AI search.
And what we can start to do is we can start to create content that approaches a topic from all these different angles.
So that we can really position our clients the way they want in the answers, right?
So with augment code.
But they care a lot about, we have this huge window for context understanding.
We're the best at massive code bases.
And so you can see that there's content we've created for them that talk about tools for different code bases and how to use context engines with AI as opposed to traditional code search.
And in this answer here, you know, they're listed as like the leading tool.
And the first five citations, seven out of 10 are all articles that we've written for them.
So we've had a lot of success working with clients like that.
And when you think about like how the content actually gets made in that strategy sprint, we are really trying to give as much context as possible.
And so Engine is in the business travel space.
And we created like these verticalized persona artifacts because for them, they wanted to create content.
And was industry specific.
And you can see how deep we go, right?
Like this constructions operations manager, what are their fears and motivations?
And how do you win or lose their trust?
All as it relates to what Engine does, right?
Business travel.
This is all connected to like frustrations around traveling.
And so we connect that to why they choose Engine.
And then we go deep into the finance manager's world, the project manager's world.
And so with every piece of content we create, we've got case studies as an artifact here.
We're able to leverage this to really make the content specific to the brand that we're working with.
How do we mention competitors?
What are the specific terminology that we should use that resonates?
How many words should be hyperlinked?
It can be very specific.
you're building all of this super detailed information based on input that you're getting.
From the company and your own research about from competitors in the space?
Exactly.
Best practices, if we don't get as much input, can be where we generate these artifacts from without your help.
But what's nice, I think, is we can start off creating these and then get input from you that informs how we flesh out things like this section, right?
What's the terminology?
What's the precise language that we should and shouldn't use?
What's a good example and a bad example?
We can come prepared with a starting point.
But then what's helpful is being able to get your insight into, okay, like we know we don't want to mention competitors in this way.
Or this is a term that, you know, really like would not resonate to the technical audience that we're going after.
So we always have a starting point, you know, but collaboration definitely makes things as personalized as they can possibly be.
Yeah.
So doing those, the thing.
The first eight weeks, we'll be creating it.
I will provide input and I will also review everything and provide feedback so we can make sure that this is accurate.
Okay.
Exactly.
Exactly.

And then when it comes to creating an article, you know, Lovable, we've built out their templates library and we like connect to their products to actually create these app templates where each page, you know, is optimized in terms of the text.
And so this is like more of a, you know, custom use case, but we do the bread and butter editorial for them.
And the way that we come up with this content, just to give you an idea, you know, we set up these pipelines.
I'm sure you've seen like a little bit of this if you saw the workshop.
But what's great about this is, you know, our inputs are leveraging these artifacts and then instructions from our writing experts so that when it actually comes to using, you know, specialized...

tools for research, in addition to open AI and anthropic models, we can actually go in and generate research where we're basically fine-tuning it for 30 minutes, looking at different sources, running it in a loop if we don't hit a certain quality score.
with each step of the process, we really build on itself, right?
So the drafting stage, we're basically using the writing guidelines, and then after we've drafted the article, we're validating against the writing guidelines.
After we've written the article, we're fact-checking and doing deep research again on every single passage in the article.
And so this shows you, like, every step that's happening, and there's obviously, like, unique inputs for each step in the process.
But if you look into our workflow...
You know, let's take this research step, right?
It's like a little bit of a black box in terms of how it's generating the research plan, how it's evaluating the quality.
But if you look into our actual workflow here, the step-by-step activities, you know, there's a prompt that helps generate the research plan along with all those inputs.
And it's a super detailed, crazy, long prompt.
There is a way that we're synthesizing these results.
There is a way that we're evaluating the quality.
And so it's everything that you're seeing here connected to each step in the process being a custom workflow.
You know, a workflow that can have all the relevant cross-links, that can find source links to make sure that any claim, right, has a citation behind it.
it's a prompt to basically evaluate how well did this adhere to the writing guidelines?
What are the most optimized meta tags?
And URL path for the article.
What's an article image that's on brand, you know, with the guidelines that you've shared with us?
And then we do this for refreshing content.

We do this for setting up custom pipelines, like when we built these templates for Lovable or whenever we built this vendor directory for Ramp, where we use their internal data to populate each of these charts.
And so we're like flexible as a partner, but I think for early stage startups, the place where we win is doing a lot of editorial content because you need to start seeing signals.
You need to start getting content indexed and you're starting a bit from zero, right?
You've begun to drive some traffic.
starting from zero, yes.
Okay, fair enough.
Yeah, you've started driving some traffic, but yes, we do need to create a lot of content to start to get you the visibility against some of these larger competitors.
Okay.
Okay.
Um...

I know it's really hard when probably people are asking you about outcomes, but from what you've seen for companies like me that are starting from zero, how long does it take to start getting a signal?
Because what you showed me on, I don't remember their name, but the latest example, results came very quickly.
Is that what you're seeing even in companies that are starting from zero?
I would say it definitely takes longer companies starting from zero.
Augment code had the benefit of being in a space that was constantly being searched, right?
How can I use AI with coding?
But we do have a lot of series A companies that we've started with, even some seed stage companies.
So it'd probably be best to just send you some proof points and some SEMrush charts so that you can see the growth that they've had.
But to be honest, you know, at least three months, right?
least three to six months to really start seeing trends.
Ah, okay.
Okay.
Look, I'm sold.
I believe in the process.
I looked at the sessions before coming to this call.
I think this is the right mode of operation.
The only concern I had was really about handling the technical part of the content.
But if you have Abnormal and many other customers, then it seems like you found the right way to do it.
And I think that I have a lot of really good resources that I've created during my onboarding to help you hit that note.
I'd love to start.
The only question that I have is I'm fine with the 15K.
Is there any way to, you know, give me a little bit of discount for the initial fee?
Yeah, the strategy sprint.
guess, how soon could you get started with us?
How soon can you?
I mean, I'm ready.
Yeah.
And we would work with who from your team?
You and anybody else?
Me.
Is there anyone on the content team today?
No.
There's a digital marketer, but I think that because this is about positioning and getting the content right, I think it's better for me to focus on that and he can focus on the paid and social.
And there's another one working on ABM and events.
So she's definitely not the right person.
Makes sense.
Makes sense.
I think let's do this as a first step.
I'll send over our contract and then once it's through legal review on your side, then we can get a sense of like all of the areas where there still needs to be alignment.
Okay.
And then once we have a good idea of that, I can in tandem, you know, see what's possible with phase one.
But, um,
Definitely, you know, I'm going to send some examples that you can look at what we've created for some technical clients.
Please send me a couple examples of content that represents, like, the quality that you'd want to see us create at scale.
And then in tandem, we can make sure that all the terms make sense in the contract so that it's easy to decide on the path forward from there.
Perfect.
And in terms of getting started, how long, you know, how long do you think that it will get the team to get ready?
I'm assuming you need to find the right team for that and assign responsibilities.
So how long will it take you to get started?
Assuming that within a week we're getting the contract out and everything's fine.
Yeah, I think you've caught us in, like, a good place right now where we just opened up some more capacity because we've just expanded the team quite a bit.
So I think...
No promises, but Monday the 26th could be possible.
Okay, so let's start moving.
If not, it might be February 9th.
But it sounds like you're ready to go.
And if we can get the 26th, you would really prefer that.
Yeah, the sooner the better.
Perfect.
Thank you so much for your time.
really appreciate it.
And I'm looking forward to seeing the examples and the contact.
Awesome.
I'll go ahead and send that probably in like the next two to three hours.
You're based in the U.S., right?
believe Boston, if I remember correctly.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I think if you could send me some examples by this afternoon and then we could get the contract reviewed by early next week, we should be in a good place to finalize the pricing on phase one.
Amazing.
Thank you so much.
Thank you.
Great to see you, Maya.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review a potential content partnership between GrowthX AI and Daylight.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=60.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Partnership Agreed:** Daylight will partner with GrowthX AI for an end-to-end content service to build domain authority from scratch.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1408.0)
  - [**Two-Phase Engagement:** A $30k Phase 1 (8-week strategy sprint) will define the content roadmap and calibrate quality. This is followed by a $15k/month Phase 2 for scaled content production (5 articles/week).](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=494.0)
  - [**Technical Content Solved:** GrowthX AI’s model—using a proprietary platform, technical managing editors, and client input—resolved Daylight's primary concern about producing high-quality technical content.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1503.0)
  - [**Urgent Timeline:** Both parties are targeting a January 26 start date, requiring rapid contract review and information exchange.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1644.0)

## Topics

### Daylight's Content Challenge

  - [Daylight needs to build domain authority from a starting point of zero organic traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1408.0)
  - [The primary concern was ensuring the content would be technically accurate and sophisticated enough for Daylight's audience.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1503.0)

### GrowthX AI's Solution & Process

  - [**Service:** An end-to-end content partner providing strategy, production, and publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=71.0)
  - [**Team:** 90+ person company with a 65-person delivery team led by Matthew Panzarino (former TechCrunch EIC).](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=233.0)
  - [**Technical Content Model:**](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
      - [**Platform:** Proprietary internal platform (not Aerobs) for custom workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=909.0)
      - [**Staffing:** A technical managing editor is assigned to each account.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
      - [**Inputs:** Client-provided resources (personas, brand guides) are used to create detailed "artifacts" (e.g., persona profiles, competitor analysis) that guide content creation.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1059.0)
  - [**Case Study (Augment Code):**](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=923.0)
      - [Grew from \~20k to 60k+ monthly organic traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=958.0)
      - [Achieved top-ranking keywords and secured multiple citations in AI search answers, demonstrating success in both traditional and AI search.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=985.0)

### Engagement Structure & Pricing

  - [**Phase 1: Strategy Sprint ($30k)**](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=494.0)
      - [**Duration:** 8 weeks.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [**Deliverables:**](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
          - [Content OS setup with keyword analysis and topic clusters.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
          - [Content roadmap.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
          - [5–10 calibration articles published to align on quality.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=606.0)
      - [**Opt-out:** Daylight can terminate the engagement after Phase 1.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=476.0)
  - [**Phase 2: Scale ($15k/month)**](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=494.0)
      - [**Production:** 5 articles per week.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
      - [**Pricing:** A $3k/month discount from the standard $18k/month rate, offered to support an early-stage startup.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=494.0)

### Collaboration & Logistics

  - [**Daylight's Role:**](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
      - [Provide initial resources (personas, brand guides, competitor examples).](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
      - [Maintain a clear, timely feedback loop to prevent production bottlenecks.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=724.0)
  - [**GrowthX AI's Role:**](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=640.0)
      - [Handle all publishing logistics: staging, meta tags, images, and infographics.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=640.0)
      - [Requires CMS access with the ability to insert tables, which are beneficial for LLM visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=790.0)
  - [**Timeline:** Organic traffic signals are expected within 3–6 months.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:** Send contract and technical client examples to Maya.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1599.0)
  - [**Maya:** Send content examples representing Daylight's desired quality.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1621.0)
  - [**Maya:** Review contract with legal by early next week to finalize Phase 1 pricing.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1714.0)
  - [**Both:** Target a January 26 start date.](https://fathom.video/share/8RQRVAXbVTTjiqFQgEDBCCUHzAz155Fo?tab=summary&timestamp=1644.0)
